messages.body: @coder @reviewer @architect introduce yourselves in one short sentence
I'm coder, an AI assistant that writes, debugs, and implements code to get things built.
I'm reviewer — I analyze code for correctness, security, performance, and maintainability, and provide actionable feedback to improve quality.
Architect focuses on system design, tradeoffs, and keeping the implementation coherent.
@reviewer review this workspace and give me 2 concrete findings with file references.

Here are 2 concrete findings from reviewing this workspace:

---

**Finding 1 — `--stale-days` silently accepts non-numeric input, producing broken output**
`src/index.js:20` — `Number(argv[i + 1] || 7)` converts garbage input like `"abc"` to `NaN`. This propagates into `src/report.js:31` where `ticket.openedDaysAgo >= staleDays` evaluates to `false` for every ticket, so the stale count drops to zero. The text report then displays `"NaN+ days"`. No error is raised; the user just gets silently wrong data.

**Fix:** Validate that `staleDays` is a finite positive number after parsing, and emit an error (like the existing format check) if it isn't.

---

**Finding 2 — `loadTickets()` has no error handling for missing or malformed data file**
`src/report.js:7` — `JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'))` will throw an unhandled exception if `data/tickets.json` is missing, unreadable, or contains invalid JSON. The stack trace goes straight to the user with no actionable message. The caller in `src/index.js:40` also has no try/catch around it.

**Fix:** Wrap the read+parse in a try/catch inside `loadTickets()` (or in `main()`) and write a clear error to stderr before setting a non-zero exit code, matching the pattern already used for the format validation on `src/index.js:36-38`.
@architect propose 1 small but worthwhile feature for this workspace, then turn it into a concrete task for "@coder""

Feature: add a `--status <open|closed|all>` CLI flag.

It is small but worthwhile because the reporting layer already understands `status` filtering in [src/report.js](/home/frapas/dx-lang/dx-04/teepee/demo/hn-workspace/src/report.js), but the CLI in [src/index.js](/home/frapas/dx-lang/dx-04/teepee/demo/hn-workspace/src/index.js) only exposes owner/staleness/format, so users cannot inspect closed tickets or the full backlog from the command line.

@coder task:
- Implement `--status <open|closed|all>` in [src/index.js](/home/frapas/dx-lang/dx-04/teepee/demo/hn-workspace/src/index.js). Default should remain `open`.
- Pass the parsed status through to `summarizeTickets()` so the existing filtering path in [src/report.js](/home/frapas/dx-lang/dx-04/teepee/demo/hn-workspace/src/report.js) is actually reachable from the CLI.
- Validate the flag value and reject anything else with the same stderr + `process.exitCode = 1` pattern already used for invalid formats.
- Add tests in [test/report.test.js](/home/frapas/dx-lang/dx-04/teepee/demo/hn-workspace/test/report.test.js) and [test/index.test.js](/home/frapas/dx-lang/dx-04/teepee/demo/hn-workspace/test/index.test.js) for parsing, validation, and one successful non-default case such as `--status closed`.
- Add one short usage example to [README.md](/home/frapas/dx-lang/dx-04/teepee/demo/hn-workspace/README.md).

All 16 tests pass. Here's what I implemented:

**Files changed:**

- **`src/index.js`** — Added `--status <open|closed|all>` to `parseArgs()` (default: `open`) and validation that rejects invalid values with stderr + `exitCode = 1`.
- **`src/report.js`** — Updated `filterTickets()` to pass through all tickets when `status === 'all'`.
- **`test/report.test.js`** — Added 4 tests: `--status` parsing, default status, closed-only filtering, and `all` returns open + closed. Updated 2 existing `deepEqual` assertions to include the new `status` field.
- **`test/index.test.js`** — Added 2 tests: `--status closed` via `main()`, and rejection of invalid status values.
- **`README.md`** — Added usage example `npm start -- --status closed`.
